heading: Authority to accept certain uncompensated services

children.heading: Authority to accept services
children.label: a Authority to accept services
children.text: Subject to subsection (b) and notwithstanding , the Chief Operating Officer or the Administrator of a facility of the Retirement Home may accept from any person voluntary personal services or gratuitous services.section 1342 of title 31
The Chief Operating Officer or the Administrator of a facility accepting the services shall notify the person offering the services of the scope of the services accepted.

children.label: A
children.text: supervise the person providing the services to the same extent as that official would supervise a compensated employee providing similar services; and

children.label: B
children.text: ensure that the person is licensed, privileged, has appropriate credentials, or is otherwise qualified under applicable laws or regulations to provide such services.

children.label: A
children.text: serve in a policymaking position of the Retirement Home; or

children.label: B
children.text: be compensated for the services by the Retirement Home.

children.heading: Authority to recruit and train persons providing services
children.label: c Authority to recruit and train persons providing services
children.text: The Chief Operating Officer or the Administrator of a facility of the Retirement Home may recruit and train persons to provide services authorized to be accepted under subsection (a).

children.heading: Status of persons providing services
children.label: d Status of persons providing services
A
children.text: Subchapter I of chapter 81 of title 5 (relating to compensation for work-related injuries).

children.label: B
children.text: Chapter 171 of title 28 (relating to claims for damages or loss).

A person providing services accepted under subsection (a) shall be considered to be an employee of the Federal Government under paragraph (1) only with respect to services that are within the scope of the services accepted.

children.label: A
children.text: the average monthly number of hours that the person provided the services, by

children.label: B
children.text: the minimum wage determined in accordance with .section 206(a)(1) of title 29

children.heading: Reimbursement of incidental expenses
children.label: e Reimbursement of incidental expenses
children.text: The Chief Operating Officer or the Administrator of a facility accepting services under subsection (a) may provide for reimbursement of a person for incidental expenses incurred by the person in providing the services accepted under subsection (a). The Chief Operating Officer or Administrator shall determine which expenses qualify for reimbursement under this subsection.
